result:
Build is green.

## Report

**File modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:30-55` (replaced the `todo!()` body at line 39).

**What `hook lua-parse::parse` now does:** `parse_stub` returns `Err(LuaError::syntax(...))` with a message naming the chunk and explaining the parser isn't wired. The protected-parser pcall converts this to `LuaStatus::Syntax`, which `lua_cli` then surfaces as a normal Lua syntax error instead of a runtime panic.

**Why not a direct call:** `lua-parse` already depends on `lua-vm`, so importing `lua_parse::parse` from `lua-vm::do_` would be a cyclic crate dependency. A clean wire-up needs either moving `protected_parser`/`f_parser` into `lua-parse` or installing a parser hook on `GlobalState` from `lua-cli` — both larger than the single-todo!() scope. (And note `lua_parse::parse` itself currently returns `LuaError::syntax(...)` as a placeholder, so a real call would be functionally identical.) The PORT NOTE in the comment documents this and points to the Phase B reconcile.

**New todo!() exposed next:** None inside this function. The next iteration will likely surface whichever todo!() the runtime hits when handling/printing the `LuaStatus::Syntax` from `load()` in `lua-cli/src/main.rs`.

**Build status:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is **green** (warnings only — pre-existing unused-Result warnings).
